 47→
 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
150→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
151→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
152→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
153→            node.typeProjectionOrNull() == null &&
154→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
155→        ) {
156→emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
157→            if (autoCorrect) {
158→                node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
159→                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
160→                        plus(indentConfig.indent)
161→                    },
162→                )
163→            }
164→        }
165→
166→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
167→            if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
168→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR &&
169→                node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
170→            ) {
171→                emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
172→                if (autoCorrect) {
173→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent))
174→                }
175→            }
176→
177→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
178→// Code below is disallowed
179→                //   @Foo1
180→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
181→                emit(
182→                    node.startOffset,
183→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
184→                    true,
185→                )
186→                if (autoCorrect) {
187→                    node
188→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
189→                        .nextLeaf()
190→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
191→                }
192→            }
193→
194→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
195→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
196→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
197→                (
198→                    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() ||
199→                        (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
200→                    ) &&
201→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
202→            ) {
203→                // Code below is disallowed
204→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
205→                // But following is allowed:
206→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
207→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
208→                val errorMessage =
209→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
210→                        "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"
211→                    } else {
212→                        "Annotation should be on a separate line"
213→                    }
214→                emit(
215→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
216→                    errorMessage,
217→                    true,
218→                )
219→                if (autoCorrect) {
220→                    node
221→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
222→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
223→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
224→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
225→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null || node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
226→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
227→                                },
228→                        )
229→                }
230→            }
231→
232→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
233→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
234→                ?.let { startOfList ->
235→                    // Code below is disallowed
236→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
237→                    // But following is allowed:
238→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
239→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
240→                    if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() || node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList()) &&
241→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
242→                    ) {
243→emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
244→                        if (autoCorrect) {
245→                            startOfList
246→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
247→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
248→                                )
249→                        }
250→                    }
251→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
252→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
253→                    ) {
254→                        node
255→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
256→                            .treeParent
257→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
258→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
259→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
260→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
261→                                if (autoCorrect) {
262→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
263→                                }
264→                            }
265→                    }
266→                }
267→        }
268→
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
270→            // Code below is disallowed
271→            //   @Foo1
272→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
273→            //   fun foo() {}
274→            emit(
275→                node.startOffset,
276→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
277→                true,
278→            )
279→            if (autoCorrect) {
280→                node
281→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
282→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283→            }
284→        }
285→    }
286→
287→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
288→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
289→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
290→            ?.treeParent
291→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
292→            ?.treeParent
293→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
294→            ?.treeParent
295→
296→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
297→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
298→        return siblings(forward = false)
299→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
300→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
301→    }
302→
303→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
304→        node: ASTNode,
305→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
306→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
307→    ) {
308→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
309→            emit(
310→                node.startOffset,
311→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
312→                true,
313→            )
314→            if (autoCorrect) {
315→                node
316→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
317→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
318→                        getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
319→                            plus(indentConfig.indent)
320→                        },
321→                    )
322→            }
323→        }
324→
325→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
326→            emit(
327→                node.startOffset,
328→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
329→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
330→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
331→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
332→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
333→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
334→            )
335→            if (autoCorrect) {
336→                node
337→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
338→                    .nextLeaf()
339→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
340→                    ?.let {
341→                        val newlineWithIndent =
342→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
343→                                plus(indentConfig.indent)
344→                            }
345→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
346→                            it.replaceWithText(newlineWithIndent)
347→                        } else {
348→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
349→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(newlineWithIndent),
350→                            )
351→                        }
352→                    }
353→            }
354→        }
355→    }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
358→
359→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
360→        psi
361→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
362→            ?.useSiteTarget
363→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
364→
365→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
366→
367→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
368→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
369→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
372→        this ==
373→            treeParent
374→                .children()
375→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
376→
377→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
378→        this ==
379→            treeParent
380→                .children()
381→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
382→
383→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
384→        siblings(forward = false)
385→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
386→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
387→
388→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
389→        siblings()
390→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
391→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
392→
393→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
394→        siblings(forward = false)
395→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
396→
397→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
398→        siblings(forward = false)
399→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
400→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
401→
402→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
403→
404→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
405→        siblings(forward = true)
406→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
407→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
408→
409→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
410→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
411→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
412→            .leaves(forward = true)
413→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
414→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
415→    }
416→
417→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
418→        val astNode =
419→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
420→                treeParent
421→            } else {
422→                this
423→            }
424→        return checkNotNull(
425→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
426→        )
427→    }
428→
429→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
430→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
431→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
432→        } else {
433→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
434→        }
435→
436→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
437→        node: ASTNode,
438→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
439→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
440→    ) {
441→        node
442→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
443→            .nextCodeLeaf()
444→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
445→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
446→
447→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
448→                    emit(
449→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
450→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
451→                        true,
452→                    )
453→                    if (autoCorrect) {
454→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
455→                    }
456→                }
457→            }
458→    }
459→
460→    private fun visitAnnotation(
461→        node: ASTNode,
462→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
463→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
464→    ) {
465→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
466→
467→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
468→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
469→        ) {
470→            emit(
471→                node.startOffset,
472→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
473→                true,
474→            )
475→            if (autoCorrect) {
476→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
477→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
478→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
479→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
480→                }
481→            }
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
486→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
487→            modifierListRoot
488→                .treeParent
489→                .treePrev
490→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
491→        val indentWithoutNewline =
492→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
493→                ?.text
494→                .orEmpty()
495→                .let { text ->
496→                    if (text.contains('\n')) {
497→                        text.substringAfterLast('\n')
498→                    } else {
499→                        modifierListRoot
500→                            .treeParent
501→                            .indent()
502→                            .takeIf { modifierListRoot.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR }
503→                            ?: text
504→                    }
505→                }
506→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
507→    }
508→}
509→
510→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
511→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→
 53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 54→
 55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 64→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 65→
 66→
 67→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 68→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 69→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
 70→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
 71→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
 72→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
 73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 75→
 76→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
 77→
 78→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 79→
 80→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
 81→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 82→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2              | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3                   |
 83→
 84→
 85→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:
 86→
 87→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`   | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`     |
 88→|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
 89→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtlintVersion   | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.KtlintVersion |
 90→
 91→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-logger`:
 92→
 93→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`               | New class/package name in `ktlint-logger`                   |
 94→|-------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
 95→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt | com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt |
 96→
 97→Module `ktlint` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli`. Classes below have been relocated in this module:
 98→
 99→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli` |
100→|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST

 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST

 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.applyIf
 47→
 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
150→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
151→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
152→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
153→            node.typeProjectionOrNull() == null &&
154→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
155→        ) {
156→emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
157→            if (autoCorrect) {
158→                node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
159→                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
160→                        plus(indentConfig.indent)
161→                    },
162→                )
163→            }
164→        }
165→
166→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
167→            if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
168→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR &&
169→                node.isFirstAnnotationEntry() &&
170→                !node.isPrecededByNewline()
171→            ) {
172→                emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
173→                if (autoCorrect) {
174→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent))
175→                }
176→            }
177→
178→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
179→// Code below is disallowed
180→                //   @Foo1
181→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
182→                emit(
183→                    node.startOffset,
184→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
185→                    true,
186→                )
187→                if (autoCorrect) {
188→                    node
189→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
190→                        .nextLeaf()
191→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
192→                }
193→            }
194→
195→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
196→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
197→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
198→                (
199→                    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() ||
200→                        (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
201→                    ) &&
202→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
203→            ) {
204→                // Code below is disallowed
205→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
206→                // But following is allowed:
207→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
208→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
209→                val errorMessage =
210→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
211→                        "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"
212→                    } else {
213→                        "Annotation should be on a separate line"
214→                    }
215→                emit(
216→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
217→                    errorMessage,
218→                    true,
219→                )
220→                if (autoCorrect) {
221→                    node
222→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
223→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
224→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
225→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
226→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null || node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
227→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
228→                                },
229→                        )
230→                }
231→            }
232→
233→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
234→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
235→                ?.let { startOfList ->
236→                    // Code below is disallowed
237→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
238→                    // But following is allowed:
239→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
240→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
241→                    if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() || node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList()) &&
242→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
243→                    ) {
244→emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
245→                        if (autoCorrect) {
246→                            startOfList
247→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
248→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
249→                                )
250→                        }
251→                    }
252→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
253→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
254→                    ) {
255→                        node
256→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
257→                            .treeParent
258→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
259→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
260→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
261→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
262→                                if (autoCorrect) {
263→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
264→                                }
265→                            }
266→                    }
267→                }
268→        }
269→
270→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
271→            // Code below is disallowed
272→            //   @Foo1
273→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
274→            //   fun foo() {}
275→            emit(
276→                node.startOffset,
277→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
278→                true,
279→            )
280→            if (autoCorrect) {
281→                node
282→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
283→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
284→            }
285→        }
286→    }
287→
288→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() =
289→        firstChildLeafOrSelf()
290→            .leaves(forward = false)
291→            .drop(1)
292→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
293→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
294→
295→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
296→takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
297→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
298→            ?.treeParent
299→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
300→            ?.treeParent
301→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
302→            ?.treeParent
303→
304→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
305→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
306→        return siblings(forward = false)
307→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
308→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
309→    }
310→
311→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
314→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
315→    ) {
316→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
317→            emit(
318→                node.startOffset,
319→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
320→                true,
321→            )
322→            if (autoCorrect) {
323→                node
324→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
325→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
326→                        getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
327→                            plus(indentConfig.indent)
328→                        },
329→                    )
330→            }
331→        }
332→
333→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
334→            emit(
335→                node.startOffset,
336→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
337→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
338→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
339→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
340→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
341→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
342→            )
343→            if (autoCorrect) {
344→                node
345→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
346→                    .nextLeaf()
347→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
348→                    ?.let {
349→                        val newlineWithIndent =
350→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
351→                                plus(indentConfig.indent)
352→                            }
353→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
354→                            it.replaceWithText(newlineWithIndent)
355→                        } else {
356→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
357→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(newlineWithIndent),
358→                            )
359→                        }
360→                    }
361→            }
362→        }
363→    }
364→
365→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
366→
367→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
368→        psi
369→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
370→            ?.useSiteTarget
371→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
372→
373→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
374→
375→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
376→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
377→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
378→
379→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
380→        this ==
381→            treeParent
382→                .children()
383→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
386→        this ==
387→            treeParent
388→                .children()
389→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
390→
391→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
392→        siblings(forward = false)
393→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
394→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
395→
396→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
397→        siblings()
398→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
399→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
400→
401→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
402→        siblings(forward = false)
403→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
404→
405→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
406→        siblings(forward = false)
407→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
408→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
409→
410→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
411→
412→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
413→        siblings(forward = true)
414→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
415→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
416→
417→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
418→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
419→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
420→            .leaves(forward = true)
421→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
422→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
423→    }
424→
425→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
426→        val astNode =
427→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
428→                treeParent
429→            } else {
430→                this
431→            }
432→        return checkNotNull(
433→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
434→        )
435→    }
436→
437→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
438→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
439→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
440→        } else {
441→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
442→        }
443→
444→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
445→        node: ASTNode,
446→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
447→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
448→    ) {
449→        node
450→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
451→            .nextCodeLeaf()
452→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
453→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
454→
455→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
456→                    emit(
457→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
458→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
459→                        true,
460→                    )
461→                    if (autoCorrect) {
462→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
463→                    }
464→                }
465→            }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun visitAnnotation(
469→        node: ASTNode,
470→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
471→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
472→    ) {
473→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
474→
475→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
476→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
477→        ) {
478→            emit(
479→                node.startOffset,
480→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
481→                true,
482→            )
483→            if (autoCorrect) {
484→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
485→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
486→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
487→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
488→                }
489→            }
490→        }
491→    }
492→
493→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
494→        val treeParent = modifierListRoot.treeParent
495→        if (treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
496→            return "\n" + treeParent.treeParent.indent()
497→        }
498→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = treeParent.treePrev
499→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
500→        val indentWithoutNewline =
501→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
502→                ?.text
503→                .orEmpty()
504→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
505→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
506→    }
507→}
508→
509→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
510→

 29→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 30→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 31→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 32→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 33→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 34→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 35→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 36→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 37→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.util.safeAs
 38→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.applyIf
 47→
 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
150→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
151→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
152→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
153→            node.typeProjectionOrNull() == null &&
154→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
155→        ) {
156→emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
157→            if (autoCorrect) {
158→                node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
159→                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
160→                        plus(indentConfig.indent)
161→                    },
162→                )
163→            }
164→        }
165→
166→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
167→            if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
168→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR &&
169→                node.isFirstAnnotationEntry() &&
170→                !node.isPrecededByNewline()
171→            ) {
172→                emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
173→                if (autoCorrect) {
174→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent))
175→                }
176→            }
177→
178→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
179→// Code below is disallowed
180→                //   @Foo1
181→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
182→                emit(
183→                    node.startOffset,
184→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
185→                    true,
186→                )
187→                if (autoCorrect) {
188→                    node
189→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
190→                        .nextLeaf()
191→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
192→                }
193→            }
194→
195→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
196→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
197→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
198→                (
199→                    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() ||
200→                        (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
201→                    ) &&
202→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
203→            ) {
204→                // Code below is disallowed
205→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
206→                // But following is allowed:
207→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
208→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
209→                val errorMessage =
210→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
211→                        "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"
212→                    } else {
213→                        "Annotation should be on a separate line"
214→                    }
215→                emit(
216→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
217→                    errorMessage,
218→                    true,
219→                )
220→                if (autoCorrect) {
221→                    node
222→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
223→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
224→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
225→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
226→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null || node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
227→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
228→                                },
229→                        )
230→                }
231→            }
232→
233→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
234→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
235→                ?.let { startOfList ->
236→                    // Code below is disallowed
237→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
238→                    // But following is allowed:
239→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
240→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
241→                    if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() || node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList()) &&
242→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
243→                    ) {
244→emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
245→                        if (autoCorrect) {
246→                            startOfList
247→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
248→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
249→                                )
250→                        }
251→                    }
252→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
253→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
254→                    ) {
255→                        node
256→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
257→                            .treeParent
258→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
259→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
260→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
261→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
262→                                if (autoCorrect) {
263→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
264→                                }
265→                            }
266→                    }
267→                }
268→        }
269→
270→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
271→            // Code below is disallowed
272→            //   @Foo1
273→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
274→            //   fun foo() {}
275→            emit(
276→                node.startOffset,
277→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
278→                true,
279→            )
280→            if (autoCorrect) {
281→                node
282→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
283→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
284→            }
285→        }
286→    }
287→
288→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() =
289→        firstChildLeafOrSelf()
290→            .leaves(forward = false)
291→            .drop(1)
292→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
293→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
294→
295→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
296→takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
297→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
298→            ?.treeParent
299→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
300→            ?.treeParent
301→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
302→            ?.treeParent
303→
304→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
305→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
306→        return siblings(forward = false)
307→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
308→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
309→    }
310→
311→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
314→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
315→    ) {
316→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
317→            emit(
318→                node.startOffset,
319→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
320→                true,
321→            )
322→            if (autoCorrect) {
323→                node
324→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
325→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
326→                        getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
327→                            plus(indentConfig.indent)
328→                        },
329→                    )
330→            }
331→        }
332→
333→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
334→            emit(
335→                node.startOffset,
336→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
337→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
338→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
339→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
340→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
341→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
342→            )
343→            if (autoCorrect) {
344→                node
345→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
346→                    .nextLeaf()
347→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
348→                    ?.let {
349→                        val newlineWithIndent =
350→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
351→                                plus(indentConfig.indent)
352→                            }
353→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
354→                            it.replaceWithText(newlineWithIndent)
355→                        } else {
356→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
357→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(newlineWithIndent),
358→                            )
359→                        }
360→                    }
361→            }
362→        }
363→    }
364→
365→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
366→
367→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
368→        psi
369→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
370→            ?.useSiteTarget
371→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
372→
373→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
374→
375→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
376→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
377→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
378→
379→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
380→        this ==
381→            treeParent
382→                .children()
383→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
386→        this ==
387→            treeParent
388→                .children()
389→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
390→
391→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
392→        siblings(forward = false)
393→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
394→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
395→
396→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
397→        siblings()
398→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
399→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
400→
401→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
402→        siblings(forward = false)
403→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
404→
405→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
406→        siblings(forward = false)
407→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
408→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
409→
410→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
411→
412→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
413→        siblings(forward = true)
414→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
415→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
416→
417→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
418→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
419→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
420→            .leaves(forward = true)
421→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
422→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
423→    }
424→
425→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
426→        val astNode =
427→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
428→                treeParent
429→            } else {
430→                this
431→            }
432→        return checkNotNull(
433→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
434→        )
435→    }
436→
437→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
438→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
439→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
440→        } else {
441→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
442→        }
443→
444→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
445→        node: ASTNode,
446→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
447→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
448→    ) {
449→        node
450→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
451→            .nextCodeLeaf()
452→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
453→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
454→
455→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
456→                    emit(
457→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
458→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
459→                        true,
460→                    )
461→                    if (autoCorrect) {
462→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
463→                    }
464→                }
465→            }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun visitAnnotation(
469→        node: ASTNode,
470→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
471→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
472→    ) {
473→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
474→
475→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
476→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
477→        ) {
478→            emit(
479→                node.startOffset,
480→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
481→                true,
482→            )
483→            if (autoCorrect) {
484→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
485→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
486→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
487→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
488→                }
489→            }
490→        }
491→    }
492→
493→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
494→        val treeParent = modifierListRoot.treeParent
495→        return if (treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
496→            "\n" + treeParent.treeParent.indent() + indentConfig.indent
497→        } else {
498→            val nodeBeforeAnnotations = treeParent.treePrev
499→            "\n" + nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text.orEmpty().substringAfterLast('\n')
500→        }
501→    }
502→}
503→
504→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
505→
