 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}
452→
453→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId

 47→/**
 48→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 49→ *
 50→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 51→ *
 52→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 53→ *
 54→ *    ```
 55→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 56→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 57→ *    ```
 58→ *
 59→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 60→ *
 61→ *    ```
 62→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 63→ *    var x: String
 64→ *    ```
 65→ *
 66→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 67→ *
 68→ *    ```
 69→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 70→ *    ```
 71→ *
 72→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 73→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 74→ *
 75→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 76→ */

 78→    StandardRule(
 79→        id = "annotation",
 80→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 81→            setOf(
 82→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 85→            ),
 86→visitorModifiers =
 87→            setOf(
 88→                RunAfterRule(
 89→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 90→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 91→                ),
 92→            ),
 93→    ) {

 95→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 96→
 97→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 98→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 99→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
100→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
101→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
102→        )
103→    }
104→
105→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
106→        node: ASTNode,
107→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
108→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
109→    ) {
110→        when (node.elementType) {
111→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
112→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
113→            }
114→            ANNOTATION -> {
115→                // Annotation array
116→                //     @[...]
117→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
118→            }
119→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
120→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
121→            }
122→        }

124→
125→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
126→        node: ASTNode,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
129→    ) {
130→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
131→
132→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
133→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
135→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
136→        ) {
137→            // Disallow:
138→            //     @Foo class Bar
139→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
140→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
141→            //     fun foo(
142→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
143→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
144→            //     ) {}

146→        }

150→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
151→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
152→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
153→        ) {
154→            emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
155→            if (autoCorrect) {
156→                node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
157→                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
158→                        plus(indentConfig.indent)
159→                    },
160→                )
161→            }
162→        }
163→
164→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
165→            if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR &&
167→                node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
168→            ) {
169→                emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
170→                if (autoCorrect) {
171→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent))
172→                }
173→            }
174→
175→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
176→// Code below is disallowed
177→                //   @Foo1
178→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
179→                emit(
180→                    node.startOffset,
181→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
182→                    true,
183→                )
184→                if (autoCorrect) {
185→                    node
186→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
187→                        .nextLeaf()
188→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
189→                }
190→            }
191→
192→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
193→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
194→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
195→                (
196→                    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() ||
197→                        (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
198→                    ) &&
199→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
200→            ) {
201→                // Code below is disallowed
202→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
203→                // But following is allowed:
204→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
205→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
206→                val errorMessage =
207→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
208→                        "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"
209→                    } else {
210→                        "Annotation should be on a separate line"
211→                    }
212→                emit(
213→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
214→                    errorMessage,
215→                    true,
216→                )
217→                if (autoCorrect) {
218→                    node
219→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
220→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
221→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
222→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
223→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null || node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
224→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
225→                                },
226→                        )
227→                }
228→            }
229→
230→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
231→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
232→                ?.let { startOfList ->
233→                    // Code below is disallowed
234→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
235→                    // But following is allowed:
236→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
237→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
238→                    if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() || node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList()) &&
239→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
240→                    ) {
241→emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
242→                        if (autoCorrect) {
243→                            startOfList
244→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
245→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
246→                                )
247→                        }
248→                    }
249→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
250→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
251→                    ) {
252→                        node
253→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
254→                            .treeParent
255→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
256→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
257→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
258→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
259→                                if (autoCorrect) {
260→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
261→                                }
262→                            }
263→                    }
264→                }
265→        }
266→
267→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
268→            // Code below is disallowed
269→            //   @Foo1
270→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
271→            //   fun foo() {}
272→            emit(
273→                node.startOffset,
274→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
275→                true,
276→            )
277→            if (autoCorrect) {
278→                node
279→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
280→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281→            }
282→        }
283→    }
284→
285→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
286→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
287→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
288→            ?.treeParent
289→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
290→            ?.treeParent
291→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
292→            ?.treeParent
293→
294→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
295→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
296→        return siblings(forward = false)
297→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
298→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
299→    }
300→
301→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
302→        node: ASTNode,
303→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
304→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
305→    ) {
306→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
307→            emit(
308→                node.startOffset,
309→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
310→                true,
311→            )
312→            if (autoCorrect) {
313→                node
314→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
315→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
316→                        getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
317→                            plus(indentConfig.indent)
318→                        },
319→                    )
320→            }
321→        }
322→
323→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
324→            emit(
325→                node.startOffset,
326→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
327→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
328→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
329→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
330→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
331→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
332→            )
333→            if (autoCorrect) {
334→                node
335→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
336→                    .nextLeaf()
337→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
338→                    ?.let {
339→                        val newlineWithIndent =
340→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
341→                                plus(indentConfig.indent)
342→                            }
343→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
344→                            it.replaceWithText(newlineWithIndent)
345→                        } else {
346→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
347→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(newlineWithIndent),
348→                            )
349→                        }
350→                    }
351→            }
352→        }
353→    }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
358→        psi
359→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
360→            ?.useSiteTarget
361→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
362→
363→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
364→
365→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
366→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
367→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
368→
369→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
370→        this ==
371→            treeParent
372→                .children()
373→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
374→
375→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
376→        this ==
377→            treeParent
378→                .children()
379→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
380→
381→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
382→        siblings(forward = false)
383→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
384→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
385→
386→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
387→        siblings()
388→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
389→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
390→
391→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
392→        siblings(forward = false)
393→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
394→
395→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
396→        siblings(forward = false)
397→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
398→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
399→
400→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
401→
402→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
403→        siblings(forward = true)
404→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
405→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
406→
407→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
408→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
409→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
410→            .leaves(forward = true)
411→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
412→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
413→    }
414→
415→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
416→        val astNode =
417→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
418→                treeParent
419→            } else {
420→                this
421→            }
422→        return checkNotNull(
423→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
424→        )
425→    }
426→
427→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
428→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
429→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
430→        } else {
431→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
432→        }
433→
434→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
435→        node: ASTNode,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
438→    ) {
439→        node
440→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
441→            .nextCodeLeaf()
442→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
443→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
444→
445→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
446→                    emit(
447→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
448→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
449→                        true,
450→                    )
451→                    if (autoCorrect) {
452→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
453→                    }
454→                }
455→            }
456→    }
457→
458→    private fun visitAnnotation(
459→        node: ASTNode,
460→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
461→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
462→    ) {
463→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
464→
465→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
466→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
467→        ) {
468→            emit(
469→                node.startOffset,
470→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
471→                true,
472→            )
473→            if (autoCorrect) {
474→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
475→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
476→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
477→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
478→                }
479→            }
480→        }
481→    }
482→
483→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
484→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
485→            modifierListRoot
486→                .treeParent
487→                .treePrev
488→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
489→        val indentWithoutNewline =
490→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
491→                ?.text
492→                .orEmpty()
493→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
494→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
495→    }
496→}
497→
498→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId

 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
150→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
151→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
152→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
153→            node.typeProjectionOrNull() == null &&
154→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
155→        ) {
156→emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
157→            if (autoCorrect) {
158→                node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
159→                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
160→                        plus(indentConfig.indent)
161→                    },
162→                )
163→            }
164→        }
165→
166→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
167→            if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
168→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR &&
169→                node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
170→            ) {
171→                emit(node.startOffset, "Annotation should be on a separate line", true)
172→                if (autoCorrect) {
173→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent))
174→                }
175→            }
176→
177→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
178→// Code below is disallowed
179→                //   @Foo1
180→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
181→                emit(
182→                    node.startOffset,
183→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
184→                    true,
185→                )
186→                if (autoCorrect) {
187→                    node
188→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
189→                        .nextLeaf()
190→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
191→                }
192→            }
193→
194→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
195→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
196→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
197→                (
198→                    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() ||
199→                        (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
200→                    ) &&
201→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
202→            ) {
203→                // Code below is disallowed
204→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
205→                // But following is allowed:
206→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
207→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
208→                val errorMessage =
209→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
210→                        "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"
211→                    } else {
212→                        "Annotation should be on a separate line"
213→                    }
214→                emit(
215→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
216→                    errorMessage,
217→                    true,
218→                )
219→                if (autoCorrect) {
220→                    node
221→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
222→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
223→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
224→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
225→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null || node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
226→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
227→                                },
228→                        )
229→                }
230→            }
231→
232→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
233→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
234→                ?.let { startOfList ->
235→                    // Code below is disallowed
236→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
237→                    // But following is allowed:
238→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
239→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
240→                    if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() || node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList()) &&
241→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
242→                    ) {
243→emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
244→                        if (autoCorrect) {
245→                            startOfList
246→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
247→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
248→                                )
249→                        }
250→                    }
251→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
252→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
253→                    ) {
254→                        node
255→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
256→                            .treeParent
257→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
258→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
259→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
260→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
261→                                if (autoCorrect) {
262→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
263→                                }
264→                            }
265→                    }
266→                }
267→        }
268→
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
270→            // Code below is disallowed
271→            //   @Foo1
272→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
273→            //   fun foo() {}
274→            emit(
275→                node.startOffset,
276→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
277→                true,
278→            )
279→            if (autoCorrect) {
280→                node
281→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
282→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283→            }
284→        }
285→    }

287→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
288→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
289→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
290→            ?.treeParent
291→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
292→            ?.treeParent
293→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
294→            ?.treeParent
295→
296→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
297→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
298→        return siblings(forward = false)
299→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
300→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
301→    }
302→
303→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
304→        node: ASTNode,
305→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
306→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
307→    ) {
308→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
309→            emit(
310→                node.startOffset,
311→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
312→                true,
313→            )
314→            if (autoCorrect) {
315→                node
316→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
317→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
318→                        getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
319→                            plus(indentConfig.indent)
320→                        },
321→                    )
322→            }
323→        }
324→
325→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
326→            emit(
327→                node.startOffset,
328→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
329→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
330→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
331→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
332→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
333→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
334→            )
335→            if (autoCorrect) {
336→                node
337→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
338→                    .nextLeaf()
339→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
340→                    ?.let {
341→                        val newlineWithIndent =
342→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).applyIf(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR) {
343→                                plus(indentConfig.indent)
344→                            }
345→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
346→                            it.replaceWithText(newlineWithIndent)
347→                        } else {
348→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
349→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(newlineWithIndent),
350→                            )
351→                        }
352→                    }
353→            }
354→        }
355→    }

392→
393→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =

397→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
398→        siblings(forward = false)

401→
402→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
403→
404→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
405→        siblings(forward = true)
406→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }

409→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
410→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
411→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
412→            .leaves(forward = true)
413→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
414→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
415→    }

417→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
418→        val astNode =
419→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
420→                treeParent
421→            } else {
422→                this
423→            }
424→        return checkNotNull(
425→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
426→        )
427→    }

429→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
430→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {

436→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
437→        node: ASTNode,
438→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
439→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
440→    ) {
441→        node
442→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
443→            .nextCodeLeaf()
444→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
445→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
446→
447→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
448→                    emit(
449→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
450→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
451→                        true,
452→                    )
453→                    if (autoCorrect) {
454→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
455→                    }
456→                }
457→            }
458→    }
459→
460→    private fun visitAnnotation(
461→        node: ASTNode,
462→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
463→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
464→    ) {
465→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
466→
467→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
468→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
469→        ) {
470→            emit(
471→                node.startOffset,
472→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
473→                true,
474→            )
475→            if (autoCorrect) {
476→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
477→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
478→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
479→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
480→                }
481→            }
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
486→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
487→            modifierListRoot
488→                .treeParent
489→                .treePrev
490→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
491→        val indentWithoutNewline =
492→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
493→                ?.text
494→                .orEmpty()
495→                .let { text ->
496→                    if (text.contains('\n')) {
497→                        text.substringAfterLast('\n')
498→                    } else {
499→                        modifierListRoot
500→                            .treeParent
501→                            .indent()
502→                            .takeIf { modifierListRoot.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR }
503→                            ?: text
504→                    }
505→                }
506→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
507→    }
508→}

tool_calls.output_content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→
 53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 54→
 55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 64→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 65→
 66→
 67→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 68→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 69→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
 70→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
 71→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
 72→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
 73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 75→
 76→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
 77→
 78→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 79→

 81→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

 84→
 85→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:

 90→
 91→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-logger`:

 96→
 97→Module `ktlint` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli`. Classes below have been relocated in this module:
